input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML â€” use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#0078D4, Dark=#3A96DD}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework re-evaluates every `AppThemeBinding` automatically when the OS theme changes â€” no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent â€” `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
this.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,          // Light value
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // Dark value

welcomeLabel.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black, Colors.White);

myButton.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#0078D4"),
    Color.FromArgb("#3A96DD"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Reacts to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **No manual subscription** | Both approaches auto-update on OS theme change |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For larger apps, define colors in `AppTheme`-aware `ResourceDictionary` styles instead of inline values |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting |

For a real app, prefer pulling colors from a shared `ResourceDictionary` with `AppThemeBinding` so you have a single place to manage your palette.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for help with .NET MAUI theming using `AppThemeBinding`. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change task in a repository. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
